subject: Can you give me a minute to explain?

body:
I'm keeping this brief.

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-june?refcode=rtw_20190609_em&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=") Team, In a moment, I'm going to ask you to make a contribution to my re-election campaign, but first, let me explain why this is so important. Right now, we're running behind our June fundraising goal. These goals aren't arbitrary -- they're carefully calculated based on the resources we'll need to respond to GOP attacks (which have already started!) and organize across Minnesota to win this race. **Missing these goals means immediate cuts to our program: fewer organizers, fewer television and digital ads, fewer opportunities to connect with voters. And with the GOP already targeting us for defeat, we cannot afford to let that happen.** Minnesota was one of the closest states in 2016, and Trump has made clear it will be one of his top targets in 2020. Pro-Trump super PACs have already spent tens of thousands in attack ads against me, and the GOP is recruiting right-wing candidates to defeat me. If we fall short now, we'll lose this race -- and we'll lose Democrats' opportunity to take back the Senate and protect our Supreme Court for a generation. **That 's why I'm personally asking for your help to keep this campaign going strong and help me keep fighting for our Minnesota values. subject: Important Election in Virginia Tomorrow!
body:
| | Peter, What if every person of faith voted? Millions of Christians in your state tomorrow have the opportunity to bring faith to the ballot box and stand for biblical principles. Imagine the impact Christians can have by voting for godly leaders. Unborn lives can be saved, religious freedom upheld, and marriage and families protected throughout the state. **Tomorrow, June 11, is the Virginia Primary election of the house of delegates and state senate.** Please take the time to learn about the candidates on your ballot with [iVoterGuide](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=oVgJzZUfsWrMNLO-1KDI_nOQkT-4sn0Tmv0FPNBbPxMdjrCIq570sl_i4pFsq9xK&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190610gotv_db4_va&n=1). subject: This baffles me

body:

---
| **IMMIGRATION UPDATE:**
Democrats pass amnesty bill while _border crisis rages_
---

* * *

Friend,

House Democrats just passed another open border bill, knowing full well there's a fat chance it'll survive in the Senate, especially if I have any say in it.

Instead of focusing on voters' border security agenda, Democrats decided to pass the DREAM Act, which strengthens amnesty protections for illegal immigrants and provides ZERO funding for border security.

[**Friend, this is just a sneak peek of what's to come if Democrats gain ground in 2020, and we've got to do everything we can to stop them now.**](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/51854280:Wy7qsCwNW:m:1:2159797350:9981F23F8440BE058D384FA72A1B791E:r)

[ DEFEAT THE DEMS ⇒
](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/51854280:Wy7qsCwNW:m:1:2159797350:9981F23F8440BE058D384FA72A1B791E:r)
By now, I shouldn't be surprised by any of this, but the Left's open border agenda still baffles me. We're in the midst of a full-blown immigration crisis and Democrats are consciously making it much worse.

**Not only do they refuse to work with Republicans on immigration solutions - they're actively working to obstruct common-sense solutions.**

I recently reintroduced legislation that focuses on raising wages for hard-working Americans and prioritizes high-skilled immigrants - but the concept of a merit-based immigration system is too "extreme" for Democrats.

For now, House Democrats' latest amnesty attempt is on the road to nowhere as long as we hold on to our GOP Senate majority. But if they turn the Senate blue, the Democrats' DREAM may become our nightmare.

[**Let's make sure that doesn't happen.**](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/51854280:Wy7qsCwNW:m:1:2159797350:9981F23F8440BE058D384FA72A1B791E:r)

In Service,

name: Andrew Yang
subject: The Need for Solutions
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
| | Friend --
[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=061019_newsletter_2&n=1) Last night, I spoke at the Iowa Democratic Party Hall of Fame celebration. I was honored to be included with so many other candidates in celebrating the induction of Iowans who have been fighting to make their state a better place. I listened carefully to what all the other candidates were saying. I heard a lot of attacks against Donald Trump, but not a lot of ideas on how to address the fundamental problems that got him elected. The truth is that we automated away over 4 million manufacturing jobs in states across the US, including Iowa, Wisconsin, Pennsylvania, Michigan, and the other swing states that Trump needed to win and did win. We're in the middle of the biggest economic and technological shift in the history of the world - experts are calling it the Fourth Industrial Revolution - and our politicians are asleep at the wheel. As automation takes away more and more jobs at a faster and faster pace, our leaders are proposing 20th Century solutions to 21st Century problems. We need to evolve our economy, and we need to do it fast. Google has already demonstrated software that can take the place of call center workers. Uber, Lyft, and Tesla are putting self-driving cars on the roads today. McDonald's is replacing cashiers with self-service kiosks in all of their locations by 2020. Bain and McKinsey estimate that 30-50% of jobs can be automated within the next decade. And the list of jobs included surprises many \- it includes radiologists, lawyers, and pharmacists. Software has been shown to be much better at detecting tumors than humans, as they can use tens of thousands of prior diagnoses as reference points almost instantaneously. My friends are already automating away much of the work that first-year lawyers can do. As someone who worked as a corporate attorney for five months, it's not a surprise to me that legal work can be automated. I've looked at the solutions proposed by other candidates, and, quite frankly, they won't work. One common talking point is we need to retrain Americans for the jobs of the future, but our government retraining programs have an effectiveness rate of between 0-15%. Others say we need to offer free college, but only one out of three Americans have college degrees, and the underemployment rate for recent college grads is 44%. A jobs guarantee might be the most ambitious proposal, but it's also the most outlandish. Companies start with an idea of what they want to accomplish, and then they hire to make that happen. Starting with a number of employees and then deciding what to do with them would be expensive, inefficient, and dehumanizing. There's also evidence that people would have difficulty transitioning from a job created under this program to other, non-government jobs, since working a guaranteed job isn't seen as a sign of skill. And the costs and new bureaucracy of administering such a program would be astronomical. The easiest way to help Americans transition through this fundamental shift in the way our economy functions is to provide them with a Freedom Dividend of $1,000/month. This would allow each American, and every community, to decide how best to make this transition. Some would spend it on training programs that best fit their needs. Others would pay off bills and ensure that they were on stable economic footing in order to survive through a job transition. Others would move for work, or buy a car so they could commute, or even take some time off to care for a newborn or ailing family member. The Freedom Dividend would give people the freedom to make this decision for themselves. It's a modern solution for a modern problem. It will evolve our economy to the new reality that is coming crashing down on us in the form of AI and automation. And it would respect the ingenuity and hard-working nature of all Americans who just want to build a better lives for themselves, their families, and their communities. The more I hear the solutions being proposed by other candidates, the more I know I have to keep fighting for the Freedom Dividend. [**Please help me do that by donating $20 today.**](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_4EuiRHvEQu_OzjBiZlAGztwOurmyDQdaH3NaKLwEpcOeLHcr_ZoyjmBbfdAE_htA3NMKjWKgbEQ19_kNpni9T_zZKIHjJ_7ivr3WfGwkVdZZAo4di2NGhsKbkQ9lOAPrLw7wMhTHlXl_VI1112M2djHGqMp1j7I1Xyf4Bpn4eOlAk4HHKAk0w5i0cervvCQGQ&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=061019_newsletter_2&n=2 "ActBlue Page for Andrew Yang") Your grateful candidate, -Andrew

**Join the Yang Gang!**

| Derek,
The Democratic primary is officially upon us, with Democrat presidential hopefuls passing through our state everyday. They are gaining free media coverage every day in New Hampshire. We must fight back with our message.
The Trump Victory ground operation is ramping up and you may have even already attended a training or house meeting, which is great! We are looking for Republican community leaders to help with Counter demonstrations. These fun, usually themed events are a chance to come together and have a good time and a good laugh while pushing back on Democrats' socialist agenda.
These events vary in time and day, as they are planned around the Democrats’ schedules, but if you are interested we can let you know when we are doing a demonstration near you
Also, if you have a hard time making it out to our events during the week you can still help by submitting an op-ed or a LTE to your local paper. This is one of the easiest ways you can help, and also one of the places we need the most help. name: Andrew Gillum
subject: you can feel it in the air
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Forward Florida Action,
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3583/9e00690/6a0474af/240779f9/342144717/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fwd-fla-action?refcode=em-header-logo&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=061019-es-fired-up-pet-nfr&source=061019-es-fired-up-pet-nfr")

You could feel the excitement in the air this past weekend when I visited the fine folks of the Villages and fired up Democrats from across the state during the Florida Democratic Party's Leadership Blue weekend.

While people may have different ideas about how we move the ball down the court, one theme was echoed by everyone I had the pleasure of meeting and talking with: the only way we can move Florida and our nation forward is by working together.
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3583/9e00690/6a0474af/240779f8/342144717/VEsE/ "https://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/forward?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=061019-es-fired-up-pet-nfr&source=061019-es-fired-up-pet-nfr")

_This packed room of Democrats in the Republican stronghold of the VIllages are fired up and more motivated than ever to help turn Florida blue, and defeat Donald Trump in 2020._
Everyone I spoke with knows how much is at stake in this election, and that we simply can't afford to have Donald Trump in the White House for four more years. So much damage has already been done it will take years to clean up, and we must work together to ensure he spends the rest of his days on the golf course, not continuing to play games with our future.

**There's only one way we can do that, team.**

**[We've got to put in the work to register and reengage a million voters in Florida. That's how we turn Florida blue and defeat Donald Trump in 2020, and I hope you will join me on this mission.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3583/9e00690/6a0474af/240779fb/342144717/VEsF/ "https://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/forward?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=3+-+Weve+got+to+put+in+the+work+to+register+&utm_campaign=061019-es-fired-up-pet-nfr&source=061019-es-fired-up-pet-nfr")**

[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3583/9e00690/6a0474af/240779fa/342144717/VEsC/ "https://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/forward?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=061019-es-fired-up-pet-nfr&source=061019-es-fired-up-pet-nfr")

_The energy from folks all across the state who came to Orlando this past weekend for the Florida Democratic Party's Leadership Blue event was inspiring, and this energy is going to lift us to victory._
We are going to give people hope who have lost it along the way. We are going to give people who don't believe in voting the faith that their voice is powerful. We are going to reach out to folks who think that no one is looking out for them and tell them that we are, and we are fighting for them.

[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3583/9e00690/6a0474af/240779fd/342144717/VEsD/ "https://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/forward?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=5&utm_campaign=061019-es-fired-up-pet-nfr&source=061019-es-fired-up-pet-nfr")

_Florida Democrats at Leadership Blue are fired up and ready to flip Florida blue._
**[I'll take the arrows, the name-calling, and all the attacks they throw at us - as long as I know you are with me to organize this state, turn out our voters, and committed to helping move our nation forward.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3583/9e00690/6a0474af/240779fc/342144717/VEsA/ "https://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/forward?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=6+-+Ill+take+the+arrows+the+namecalling+and+&utm_campaign=061019-es-fired-up-pet-nfr&source=061019-es-fired-up-pet-nfr")**

There is no doubt that powerful people and outside groups want to stop us, but we're not going to let them.

We're going to flip Florida blue and our nation blue, because if we're in this together, there's nothing that will stop us.

Forward.

Andrew Gillum

subject: President Trump and America Are Winners by Getting Mexico to Intensify Fight Against Illegal Immigration
body:
"Against nearly insurmountable odds, President Trump proved his critics wrong once again by getting Mexico to agree Friday to step up its efforts..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=beb7544347&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump and America Are Winners by Getting Mexico to Intensify Fight Against Illegal Immigration**

“Against nearly insurmountable odds, President Trump proved his critics wrong once again by getting Mexico to agree Friday to step up its efforts to reduce illegal immigration to the U.S.,” National Border Patrol Council President Brandon Judd writes in Fox News.

“This win by our president shows he understands that politely asking other countries to change their behavior when it harms us isn’t always enough . . . Facing the largest border security crisis in the history of the United States, and knowing the Mexican economy is completely reliant on U.S. trade, Trump pulled out all the stops.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d72a602e1a&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Ivanka Trump brought her bid to help level the global financial playing field for 50 million women to The Hague Wednesday, where she announced a deal with Mastercard to expand capital to minorities around the world,” Paul Bedard reports for the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3ca613e5e5&e=3ee4985eea). “Investing in women is just smart business, and we should all be doing it,” Ms. Trump told the 9th Global Entrepreneurship Summit.
---
|
---
| “Mexican military deployed throughout the country as part of President Andrés Manuel López Obrador's vow to do more to apprehend migrants headed to the United States, have officially begun targeting foreigners who have hitched rides aboard a freight train known as ‘The Beast’ to get to the U.S.-Mexico border,” Anna Giaritelli reports for the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3b56b41503&e=3ee4985eea). “Riding on top of the 12-foot-tall freight cars is dangerous. Injuries and deaths have been well-documented over the past decade.”
---
|
---
| “There’s an important point to remember in the current U.S.-China trade conflict: Beijing started it. For the past 20 years, China has been at war with the U.S. economy, using massive subsidies, currency manipulation and intellectual property theft to continually erode America’s manufacturing base,” former CEO of Nucor steel company Dan Dimicco writes in [_The Hill_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a62a463400&e=3ee4985eea). “It’s a strategy that has worked phenomenally well. Over the past two decades, the United States has lost 5 million manufacturing jobs and nearly 90,000 factories. After years of this economic aggression, the Trump administration is now saying, ‘Enough is enough.’”
---
|
---
| “The first report of the impact on the bipartisan criminal justice reform package pushed by top Trump aide Jared Kushner shows that it is working to cut sentences, especially for black prisoners,” Paul Bedard reports in the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=47bdc1ba7c&e=3ee4985eea). “91.3% of those granted a cut in prison time were African American.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=054fa8fb06&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d53105b0eb&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=540956e50f)
---

name: Kamala Harris
subject: We have a winning case
body:
As a
prosecutor, I took an oath to pursue justice for the people -- and for my
entire career, I have only ever had one client: the people.

I took on the banks who defrauded homeowners, oil companies that polluted
California, pharmaceutical companies who scammed seniors, and those who
violently victimized the most vulnerable in our communities.

Right now, we have a President of the United States, a man who took an
oath to defend the Constitution, who violates that sworn promise.

We need to hold this president accountable by prosecuting the case in
front of the American people against four more years of this
Administration. If you’re with me, I need you to pitch in a contribution
to our campaign right now:

We're not expected to run for President -- and we're certainly not supposed to be frontrunners. **But I want kids of every race, creed, and religion to look up on that debate stage and know that one day, they can be President, too.** [_**Which is why sharing my story and vision in the next Democratic debates is so important to me. But I need support from 130,000 Democrats to make the cut. I've watched their divisive politics erode the promise of this country and leave everyday Americans behind. I'm running for President to put people first -- and I'm not asking permission to do it. \-- **My People First Immigration Plan** uses 21st century tools to keep our borders safe and puts Trump's cruel, heartless family separation policies behind us. \-- **My People First Education Plan** invests in our nation's teachers, and gives every student access to a quality education through their college years, ensuring our country's children -- our most precious resources -- have a strong foundation for success and opportunity. \-- **My People First Policing Plan** begins to heal the divide between communities and law enforcement and puts an end to over-aggressive and racially discriminatory policing. I'll be blunt: No one person can solve our problems. **But in times of crisis, we must strive to come together as a nation and ensure our people -- not our political interests -- are the catalyst for change. ** [_**Before midnight, I'm asking you to add your name in support of this vision for our great country and help me share it in the next Presidential debates. name: Rob Flaherty, Beto for America
subject: I need to be upfront
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
#
Hey -- Beto’s digital director here. This is the first time you’ve heard from me (hi!), but I’m reaching out now because I’ve got something important to tell you.
You know and I know that ~~the narrative~~ is mostly garbage. We’re seeing energy and growth all across the country, and a poll just a few days ago showed that we’re the most viable candidate to win the Texas primary (and if you win Texas, you can damn near win the whole thing).
With that said, I’d be lying if I said we didn’t have work to do, **and the thing we need the most right now is irrefutable fundraising numbers at the end of the month.** Emails like this one are how we raise about half of the money required to cover our costs, but only about 1-in-100 people reading any given email end up donating. That’s why I’m asking:
[Can Beto count on you to pitch in $3 before the FEC deadline so we can post strong numbers and generate the resources to compete?](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17696?refcode=ema20190610-35727&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=19&akid=35727%2E444211%2ENR_DJ8)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17696?refcode=ema20190610-35727&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=35727%2E444211%2ENR_DJ8)**
With your help, we’ve been able to break records in the past, but I look at the numbers every day, and...let’s just say that the official FEC deadline is coming up a whole lot faster than I would like.
If you need a reminder, this campaign is 100% funded by people. Beto made that choice because he believes, deeply, that it’s time to break the chokehold that PACs, corporations, and special interests have over our democracy. But that choice comes with a cost:
**We depend**** _a ton_****on email fundraising to keep up -- which is why the fact that you’re reading this email matters so much.**
I wasn’t here for the Senate race, but I was a (recurring!!) donor. Everyone counted Beto out, but with our help, he proved everyone wrong. We find ourselves there again: down, but not out, and needing to prove everyone wrong.
I mean it when I say this: we have a real shot at winning this primary. I wouldn’t have moved my life to El Paso if I didn’t think that. But we can only do it if you’re with us. So let’s get after this, with all of our strength.
[Add a $3 contribution now to push Beto forward ahead of the FEC deadline.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17696?refcode=ema20190610-35727&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=35727%2E444211%2ENR_DJ8)
Thank you for everything so far,
Rob Flaherty
Digital Director, Beto for America
**[Contribute](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17696?refcode=ema20190610-35727&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=22&akid=35727%2E444211%2ENR_DJ8)**

Paid for by Beto for America
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This campaign is powered by grassroots supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=23&akid=35727%2E444211%2ENR_DJ8). To contribute via check, please address to Beto for America campaign, P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923.

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: [Petition] Please, add your name and help me fight for equal pay for women
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
Fifty six years ago today, President Kennedy signed the Equal Pay Act, mandating that employers pay men and women equal wages in positions of "equal skill, effort, and responsibility, and which are performed under similar working conditions."
| | Peter,

Fifty six years ago today, President Kennedy signed the Equal Pay Act, mandating that employers pay men and women equal wages in positions of "equal skill, effort, and responsibility, and which are performed under similar working conditions." **But although the Equal Pay Act went into effect more than half a century ago, our country still has not reached the standard that was written into law in 1963.** As the current numbers stand, white women still only earn 80 cents, on average, for every dollar a man earns. That disparity is even wider for women of color: African American women earn 61 cents, Latina women earn 53 cents, and Native Hawaiian and Pacific Islander women earn 62 cents for every dollar a white man earns.

A woman who works full-time year-round can earn more than $400,000 __less__ than her male counterparts over the course of her career. That loss affects everything from a woman's ability to afford quality health care for herself and her family, to take out a mortgage on a house, and to save up for retirement.

[**I need your help, Peter, in the fight for equal pay for equal work. Please, add your name to my petition calling for action to close the gender pay gap and end systemic workplace discrimination. >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/VFQOFgDzAe3sSg5dCb0edYjQDdHbakQgHSnJKhbkfAA=/YJnYb1eYy_elQF9XSN5rkzfWhxsLRH6JNq4J51-nM_g=) | **[SIGN IMMEDIATELY »](https://act.myngp.com/el/VFQOFgDzAe3sSg5dCb0edYjQDdHbakQgHSnJKhbkfAA=/YJnYb1eYy_elQF9XSN5rkzfWhxsLRH6JNq4J51-nM_g=)**
---
**Let me be clear, Peter: Equal pay is not a partisan issue -- it 's an issue that affects families across VA-07 and the country.**
Our House took action to stop systemic wage discrimination for this generation and future generations of women. The **Paycheck Fairness Act** \-- a bill I cosponsored and passed in late March -- will empower women to negotiate for equal pay by giving them the tools needed to push back against discrimination in the workplace.

This is much-needed legislation, Peter, but it's one of the nearly 100 bills in Mitch McConnell's legislative graveyard. Without action in the Senate, the Paycheck Fairness Act -- _and millions of women 's paychecks_ \-- will hang in limbo.

[**Peter, on the anniversary of the Equal Pay Act becoming law, I 'm asking for your support to close the gender wage gap and guarantee equal pay for equal work. Will you sign my petition calling on the Senate to pass the Paycheck Fairness Act? >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/VFQOFgDzAe3sSg5dCb0edYjQDdHbakQgHSnJKhbkfAA=/YJnYb1eYy_elQF9XSN5rkzfWhxsLRH6JNq4J51-nM_g=)
**[SIGN IMMEDIATELY »](https://act.myngp.com/el/VFQOFgDzAe3sSg5dCb0edYjQDdHbakQgHSnJKhbkfAA=/YJnYb1eYy_elQF9XSN5rkzfWhxsLRH6JNq4J51-nM_g=)**
---
Thank you for taking action for the women and families of our district.

_\-- Abigail _

| Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative for Virginia's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. clean_body: Fifty six years ago today, President Kennedy signed the Equal Pay Act, mandating that employers pay men and women equal wages in positions of "equal skill, effort, and responsibility, and which are performed under similar working conditions." | | Peter, Fifty six years ago today, President Kennedy signed the Equal Pay Act, mandating that employers pay men and women equal wages in positions of "equal skill, effort, and responsibility, and which are performed under similar working conditions." But although the Equal Pay Act went into effect more than half a century ago, our country still has not reached the standard that was written into law in 1963. As the current numbers stand, white women still only earn 80 cents, on average, for every dollar a man earns. That disparity is even wider for women of color: African American women earn 61 cents, Latina women earn 53 cents, and Native Hawaiian and Pacific Islander women earn 62 cents for every dollar a white man earns. A woman who works full-time year-round can earn more than $400,000 less than her male counterparts over the course of her career. That loss affects everything from a woman's ability to afford quality health care for herself and her family, to take out a mortgage on a house, and to save up for retirement. = | = Let me be clear, Peter: Equal pay is not a partisan issue -- it 's an issue that affects families across VA-07 and the country. Our House took action to stop systemic wage discrimination for this generation and future generations of women. The Paycheck Fairness Act \-- a bill I cosponsored and passed in late March -- will empower women to negotiate for equal pay by giving them the tools needed to push back against discrimination in the workplace. This is much-needed legislation, Peter, but it's one of the nearly 100 bills in Mitch McConnell's legislative graveyard. Without action in the Senate, the Paycheck Fairness Act -- and millions of women 's paychecks \-- will hang in limbo. = = Thank you for taking action for the women and families of our district. \-- Abigail | Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative for Virginia's 7th Congressional District. body:
Tomorrow is Election Day and Yasmine Taeb, Our Revolution's endorsed candidate for State Senate District 35 needs your vote!
# [](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a4b7/bed43eb/9313112f/75fab8d0/3514775278/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-header-OR")
Dear Friends,
Tomorrow is Election Day and Yasmine Taeb, Our Revolution's endorsed candidate for State Senate District 35 needs your vote!
This race is looking to be a historic one building on many fronts. Yasmine Taeb is the first Muslim woman to sit on the Democratic National Committee, is the first primary challenger incumbent Senate Minority Leader Dick Saslaw since 1979, and is running during an election that could see a Democratic Senate majority for the first time in five years.
Taeb is an Our Revolution member and supported by a broad range of national and progressive groups. A refugee herself, she now works with torture victims and would bring a valuable new voice for the oppressed to Richmond. Dick Saslaw, on the other hand, comes from the Good Old Boy politics of the Commonwealth, receiving hundreds of thousands from dirty energy companies. With his leadership position, he represents the single biggest roadblock to progressive legislation landing on the governor's desk if the Democrats take control of the Senate.

Mitch McConnell just pledged if there's a Supreme Court vacancy in 2020, he'll fill it with a right-wing judge -- despite his refusal to seat President Obama's Supreme Court nominee in the 2016 election year.

It's an egregious abuse of power.

[**That 's why I'm emailing and asking for $19 to flip my must-win Colorado Senate seat and oust Mitch McConnell for good. >>**](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb8262c/232dfd1/94ef2cd/62a17c1c/1198213098/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/baer-email?refcode=20190610_em_fr_b1_baer_all&amount=19&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")

He's spent the past three years fast-tracking a record number of conservative judges to lifetime appointments on lower courts.

And now, he's promised to confirm Trump's nominee to the Supreme Court if there's a vacancy in the 2020 presidential election year -- simply because this time around, a Republican sits in the White House.

Team,
This campaign is about people like you.

name: Team Bennet
subject: Republicans are chomping at the bit
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for America
body:
| |
---
| Friend,
---
It's crucial that we win back the White House in 2020. But just as important to making change is making sure we keep strong Democrats fighting in Congress.

While most people's instincts are to freeze or run the other way when disaster strikes, Jason Crow has always gone where the fight is. From serving in the U.S. Army after 9/11 to running for Congress following Donald Trump's election, he has always stood up to defend American values.

Thanks to grassroots supporters like you, Jason did what no other Democrat has done before last November: defeat Republican Mike Coffman in the race for Colorado's 6th Congressional District.

[But, due to the high profile of his race in 2018, Republicans are already chomping at the bit to take back Jason's seat in 2020.](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cba248/134673f2/2034781/6996f53d/4116394652/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bennet-crow?refcode=EM_FR_B2_061019_AC&recurring=auto&amount=5&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000")

The top GOP super PAC and out-of-state special interest groups have placed Jason square at the top of their list of targets and are already running attack ads.
**[If we want to keep a servant leader like Jason fighting for the people in Washington, D.C., then it's on all of us to have his back and help him win re-election. So today we're asking you to split a $10 donation evenly between John and Michael. Will you chip in right now?](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cba248/134673f2/2034781/6996f53d/4116394652/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bennet-crow?refcode=EM_FR_B2_061019_AC&recurring=auto&amount=5&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000")**
[](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cba248/134673f2/2034781/6996f53c/4116394652/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bennet-crow?refcode=EM_FR_B2_061019_AC&recurring=auto&amount=5&amounts=20,35,50,100,250,500,1000")
When Jason decided to run for Congress two years ago, he promised to give regular people a voice in our democracy again and refused to take corporate PAC donations throughout his entire campaign -- a promise Jason has kept.

But, the Republicans don't feel the same way -- so we can bet whoever the GOP finds to run against Jason will have the full support of deep-pocketed billionaires and a war chest full of special interest money.
[**We cannot let this seat be stolen back by dark money. So please, chip in $10 or more to keep Jason Crow, a proven servant leader, fighting for Colorado values in D.C.**](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cba248/134673f2/2034781/6996f533/4116394652/VEsC/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bennet-crow?refcode=EM_FR_B2_061019_AC&recurring=auto&amount=3&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000")

Team Bennet

subject: How President Trump outsmarted the ‘experts’—again
body:
Eleven days ago, President Donald J. Trump gave Mexico a choice. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2fd0db5ed6&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • June 10, 2019
---
| | |
# **How President Trump outsmarted the ‘experts’—again**

**Eleven days ago,** President Donald J. Trump gave Mexico a choice: either restore the rule of law on its side of the border and help curb soaring illegal immigration into America, or face a 5 percent escalating tariff on all exports into the United States.

**A**[**Joint Declaration**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=21d6ce371d&e=3ee4985eea) between the two countries came on Friday, reading in part:
| _Mexico will take unprecedented steps to increase enforcement to curb irregular migration, to include the deployment of its National Guard throughout Mexico, giving priority to its southern border. Mexico is also taking decisive action to dismantle human smuggling and trafficking organizations as well as their illicit financial and transportation networks._
|
**With these commitments** comes renewed enforcement. Part of the new agreement, for example, requires a vote from Mexico’s legislative body to implement. “We do not anticipate a problem with the vote but, if for any reason the approval is not forthcoming, Tariffs will be reinstated!” President Trump tweeted today.

**It took only days** for President Trump to bring Mexico to the table. He did so by leveraging America’s position as the strongest economy in the world. Despite that massive negotiating advantage, many in Washington were convinced that effort would fail.

**How did the establishment** get it so wrong, yet again?

**Washington suffers** from a disease of short-term bias, most of it self-inflicted. Pundits and career politicians inflate trivial controversies to avoid addressing real ones. The mainstream media follows suit, looking for cheap sensationalism to help keep a few more viewers entertained. In both cases, the news cycle moves on before anyone is held accountable for making wrong prediction after wrong prediction.

**That short-sightedness** also tells us who the Beltway’s real customers are. It’s no coincidence that much of the political class pays closer attention to the hourly fluctuations of Wall Street than to the long-term decline of American communities. Both their jobs and those of their patrons depend on not disrupting the status quo at any cost.

**Real negotiation** , on the other hand, requires a willingness to stand your ground. It also requires understanding your bargaining power. President Trump knows that a strong America—both economically and militarily—can help shape the world for the better.

**The results** are already drowning out Washington’s “experts.” For example:
* Our NATO allies cut their defense budgets for years knowing that American taxpayers would pick up the bill. President Trump said no more—and has already secured a $100 billion _increase_ from our allies in defense spending.

* Both President Trump and former President Obama promised to renegotiate NAFTA to get a better deal for American workers. Only President Trump delivered, and the new [USMCA now awaits approval from Congress](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b8f2de8c7e&e=3ee4985eea).

* For years, pundits said that America’s days as a manufacturing powerhouse were over. Under President Trump, the U.S. economy added 284,000 manufacturing jobs in 2018—the most of any year since the 1990s.

**The** **bottom line** : American strength works. It just requires the will to use it.

[See the headlines: President Trump secures a historic immigration deal with Mexico.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cf25e9196e&e=3ee4985eea "https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/president-donald-j-trump-secures-historic-deal-mexico-combat-crisis-border/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d")
|
---
|
## Video of the Day
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d69d235fa1&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **First Lady Melania Trump** wrapped up a five-day visit to Europe with President Trump on Friday, one that included visits to the United Kingdom, Ireland, and France.

**Their trip began** with an official State Visit to the United Kingdom, where the First Lady met with Her Majesty the Queen. Throughout the visit, Mrs. Trump also attended spousal outings, including a garden party at 10 Downing Street with Mr. Philip May.

**On Thursday,** the First Lady accompanied President Trump to Normandy, France, in honor of the 75th anniversary of D-Day. The event “was a beautiful tribute to our veterans and the fallen soldiers of the D-Day invasion and the Second World War,” said Mrs. Trump. “It was an honor to be a part of this historic ceremony remembering the strength and courage of our allied forces.”

name: Senator Jeff Merkley
subject: [Petition] The dramatic plan we need to fight climate chaos
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2a6f0/6cd27893/4ccbe83b/15805987/1008522915/VEsH/ "http://www.jeffmerkley.com")
---

| | Here's a bold idea: let's dramatically cut the pollution causing a climate crisis AND create jobs and innovation here in America. How? End the sale of pollution-spewing cars by 2040. **[Sign the petition: join my call to fight climate chaos by making a total shift to zero-emissions vehicles.](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2a6f0/6cd27893/4ccbe83b/15805984/1008522915/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/ "https://www.jeffmerkley.com/petition/zero-emissions/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2019-06-zero-emissions-email")** I already drive an electric car, and it's great. Imagine never having to go to the gas station - it's liberating! Every year electric cars go further and charge faster. And there are other non-polluting technologies too. We have the technology, and it keeps getting better. So my plan is to gradually ramp up so that, by 2040, 100% of all vehicles sold in the United States are zero-emission vehicles. This matters. _A lot_. Climate chaos is _very_ _real_ and we're already feeling its devastating effects. If we don't aggressively fight it, things are only going to get much worse. And if we don't win the race to break our addiction to oil, we'll lose our edge as the center of innovation. Climate chaos is a global problem, and other countries are going gangbusters to develop solutions they can sell worldwide. We can't let climate deniers steal those economic opportunities from Americans. **[That's why I'm building a movement to address a big problem: our lingering dependence on dirty fossil fuels. And why I want you to sign my petition today telling Congress to make the total switch to zero-emission vehicles by 2040.](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2a6f0/6cd27893/4ccbe83b/15805984/1008522915/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/ "https://www.jeffmerkley.com/petition/zero-emissions/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2019-06-zero-emissions-email")** This won't be easy. clean_body: | "" | | Here's a bold idea: let's dramatically cut the pollution causing a climate crisis AND create jobs and innovation here in America. How? End the sale of pollution-spewing cars by 2040. "" I already drive an electric car, and it's great. Imagine never having to go to the gas station - it's liberating! Every year electric cars go further and charge faster. And there are other non-polluting technologies too. We have the technology, and it keeps getting better. So my plan is to gradually ramp up so that, by 2040, 100% of all vehicles sold in the United States are zero-emission vehicles. This matters. A lot. Climate chaos is very real and we're already feeling its devastating effects. If we don't aggressively fight it, things are only going to get much worse. And if we don't win the race to break our addiction to oil, we'll lose our edge as the center of innovation. Climate chaos is a global problem, and other countries are going gangbusters to develop solutions they can sell worldwide. We can't let climate deniers steal those economic opportunities from Americans. "" This won't be easy. Dramatic change never is. But it's utterly necessary. If we want to survive and thrive as a country and people, we've got to get this done. "" Onward, Jeff "" | ---|---|--- | | Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon. ---|--- | ""
